glossary.def: A vision-language model whose output space is extended to include motor commands, so a single transformer maps camera frames and a language instruction to a robot action. RT-2 emits actions as discretized tokens from the model's own vocabulary; π0 attaches a continuous action head instead.

Discretizing a continuous robot action so a token model can produce it: each action dimension is clipped to a range, split into B uniform bins, and replaced by its bin index. Decoding returns the bin center, so the round-trip error is at most half a bin width — the resolution ceiling of a binned policy.

Training a policy by supervised regression of expert actions onto the observations the expert visited — essentially LLM pretraining with actions for tokens. It is sample-efficient and stable but suffers covariate shift: the policy's own small errors push it into states no demonstration covered, where errors compound.

The imitation-learning failure in which the distribution of states a trained policy actually reaches diverges from the demonstrator's, because each action changes the next observation. Small per-step errors drift the agent into unseen states, growing roughly quadratically in the horizon; DAgger and action chunking are the standard mitigations.

Training a policy in a physics simulator and deploying it on hardware despite the reality gap — the mismatch between simulated and real dynamics, sensing, latency, and appearance. The dominant tool is domain randomization, often combined with system identification and a small amount of real-world fine-tuning.

Randomizing a simulator's parameters (masses, frictions, lighting, textures, sensor delays) so widely during training that the real world looks like just another sample. If the randomization distribution contains reality, the reality gap collapses into ordinary in-distribution generalization; too narrow overfits, too wide yields an over-conservative average policy.

A continuous alternative to action tokenization, used by π0, that learns a velocity field transporting Gaussian noise to an expert action chunk and integrates it at inference. It removes the quantization floor of binning and enables smooth high-frequency control, at the cost of a small iterative sampler instead of a single argmax.

Training a single policy on demonstrations pooled across many different robot bodies, sensors, and action spaces so each robot benefits from data it could never collect alone. Open X-Embodiment was the landmark effort, pooling roughly a million trajectories across 22 embodiments to partially offset robotics' chronic data scarcity.

refs.cite: Brohan, A. et al. (2023). RT-2: Vision-Language-Action Models Transfer Web Knowledge to Robotic Control. Google DeepMind.
Black, K. et al. (2024). π0: A Vision-Language-Action Flow Model for General Robot Control. Physical Intelligence.
Zhao, T. Z., Kumar, V., Levine, S. & Finn, C. (2023). Learning Fine-Grained Bimanual Manipulation with Low-Cost Hardware (ACT/ALOHA). RSS 2023.
Kim, M. J. et al. (2024). OpenVLA: An Open-Source Vision-Language-Action Model. CoRL 2024.
Open X-Embodiment Collaboration (2023). Open X-Embodiment: Robotic Learning Datasets and RT-X Models. ICRA 2024.
Tobin, J. et al. (2017). Domain Randomization for Transferring Deep Neural Networks from Simulation to the Real World. IROS 2017.
Ross, S., Gordon, G. & Bagnell, D. (2011). A Reduction of Imitation Learning and Structured Prediction to No-Regret Online Learning (DAgger). AISTATS 2011.
